patterns.rootCause: The {symbolKind} `{symbolName}` cannot be found. It may be misspelled, not imported, or not in scope.
patterns.prescription: Check the spelling of `{symbolName}`, add the necessary import statement, or ensure the {symbolKind} is defined and accessible in the current scope.

patterns.match: error: incompatible types: (\S+) cannot be converted to (\S+)
patterns.rootCause: Cannot convert from `{sourceType}` to `{targetType}`. The types are incompatible.
patterns.prescription: Use explicit casting if the types are related, call an appropriate conversion method, or change the variable type to match.

patterns.rootCause: The package `{packageName}` does not exist on the classpath.
patterns.prescription: Add the required dependency to your pom.xml or build.gradle, or verify the package name is correct.
patterns.codeBlock:
<!-- Maven -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.example</groupId>
    <artifactId>library</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>

patterns.rootCause: There is code after a return, throw, break, or continue statement that can never be executed.
patterns.prescription: Remove the unreachable code or restructure the control flow so that the statement is reachable.

patterns.match: error: non-static (method|variable) (\w+)\(?\)? cannot be referenced from a static context
patterns.rootCause: The non-static {memberKind} `{memberName}` cannot be accessed from a static method (e.g., main).
patterns.prescription: Create an instance of the class to access the non-static {memberKind}, or make the {memberKind} static.

patterns.rootCause: A method with a non-void return type does not return a value on all code paths.
patterns.prescription: Ensure every code path in the method returns a value. Add a return statement at the end of the method or in all branches of conditional logic.

patterns.rootCause: The class `{className}` is defined more than once in the project.
patterns.prescription: Remove the duplicate class definition, or move one of them to a different package.

patterns.match: error: (\w+) is abstract; cannot be instantiated
patterns.rootCause: The class `{className}` is abstract and cannot be directly instantiated with `new`.
patterns.prescription: Create a concrete subclass that implements all abstract methods, or use an anonymous inner class to provide the implementation inline.

patterns.rootCause: The member `{member}` has private visibility in class `{className}` and cannot be accessed from outside.
patterns.prescription: Use a public getter/setter method to access the private member, change the visibility modifier, or access it through a public API.

patterns.match: error: cannot assign a value to final variable (\w+)
patterns.rootCause: The variable `{variable}` is declared as final and cannot be reassigned after initialization.
patterns.prescription: Remove the `final` modifier if the variable needs to be reassigned, or use a different variable.

patterns.rootCause: The code uses raw types or unchecked type operations, which can lead to ClassCastException at runtime.
patterns.prescription: Add proper generic type parameters to eliminate raw type usage. Use @SuppressWarnings("unchecked") only if type safety is guaranteed by other means.

patterns.rootCause: Raw types are being used without generic type parameters, bypassing compile-time type checking.
patterns.prescription: Add generic type parameters to all collections and generic classes.

patterns.match: error: method does not override or implement a method from a supertype
patterns.rootCause: A method annotated with @Override does not actually override any method from a parent class or interface.
patterns.prescription: Verify the method signature matches the parent method exactly (name, parameters, return type). Check that the parent class or interface actually declares the method.

patterns.rootCause: The call to `{methodName}` is ambiguous because multiple overloaded methods match the provided arguments.
patterns.prescription: Cast the arguments to the specific types expected by the intended overload to disambiguate the call.

patterns.match: error: variable (\w+) is already defined in method (\w+)
patterns.rootCause: The variable `{variable}` is declared more than once in the method `{method}`.
patterns.prescription: Rename one of the duplicate variable declarations or remove the redundant declaration.

patterns.rootCause: A NullPointerException occurred: attempted to use a null reference. {message}
patterns.prescription: Add null checks before accessing the object, use Optional<T> for values that may be absent, or use Objects.requireNonNull() for early validation.

patterns.rootCause: Cannot cast `{actualType}` to `{targetType}`. The object is not an instance of the target type.
patterns.prescription: Use `instanceof` to check the type before casting, or review the code logic to ensure the correct type is being used.

patterns.match: java\.lang\.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index (\d+) out of bounds for length (\d+)
patterns.rootCause: Array index {index} is out of bounds for an array of length {length}.
patterns.prescription: Validate the index is within bounds (0 to length-1) before accessing the array. Use a bounds check or iterate with a for-each loop.

patterns.match: java\.lang\.StringIndexOutOfBoundsException: (?:String index out of range|Range \[)(\d+)(?:, (\d+)\))?(?: out of bounds for length (\d+))?
patterns.rootCause: String index out of bounds at position {index}. The string is shorter than the requested index.
patterns.prescription: Check the string length before using charAt(), substring(), or similar index-based methods.

patterns.rootCause: Division by zero occurred in an integer arithmetic operation.
patterns.prescription: Check that the divisor is not zero before performing division.

patterns.rootCause: A StackOverflowError occurred, typically caused by infinite or excessively deep recursion.
patterns.prescription: Add or fix the base case in recursive methods, convert deep recursion to iteration, or increase the stack size with -Xss JVM flag.

patterns.rootCause: The class `{className}` could not be found at runtime. It may be missing from the classpath.
patterns.prescription: Ensure the class is included in the project dependencies, verify the fully qualified class name is correct, and check that the JAR containing the class is on the classpath.

patterns.rootCause: The method `{methodSignature}` was not found at runtime, often during reflection.
patterns.prescription: Verify the method name, parameter types, and accessibility. If using reflection, ensure the method exists and use getDeclaredMethod() for private methods.

patterns.rootCause: An illegal argument was passed to a method: {message}
patterns.prescription: Validate method arguments before passing them. Check the method's documentation for acceptable parameter values and ranges.

patterns.rootCause: An operation was invoked at an illegal or inappropriate time: {message}
patterns.prescription: Ensure the object is in the correct state before calling the method. Check initialization, lifecycle state, and preconditions.

patterns.rootCause: A collection was modified while being iterated over, which is not permitted by most Java collection iterators.
patterns.prescription: Use Iterator.remove() to remove elements during iteration, use CopyOnWriteArrayList for concurrent access, or collect items to remove and process them after the loop.

patterns.rootCause: An unsupported operation was called, typically on an unmodifiable or fixed-size collection (e.g., Arrays.asList() or List.of()).
patterns.prescription: Wrap the collection in a mutable implementation if modification is needed.

patterns.rootCause: The file `{filePath}` was not found. It may not exist, or the path may be incorrect.
patterns.prescription: Verify the file path is correct, check that the file exists before accessing it, and ensure the application has read permissions.

patterns.rootCause: An I/O error occurred: {message}
patterns.prescription: Handle the IOException with try-catch or try-with-resources. Check file permissions, disk space, network connectivity, and resource availability.

patterns.rootCause: Cannot parse "{input}" as a number. The string contains non-numeric characters.
patterns.prescription: Validate the input string before parsing, use try-catch to handle invalid input, or trim whitespace from the input.

patterns.rootCause: Maven could not resolve dependencies for project `{project}`. One or more dependencies are unavailable.
patterns.prescription: Check that all dependency coordinates (groupId, artifactId, version) are correct, verify the repository URLs in pom.xml or settings.xml, and try running `mvn dependency:resolve` to identify the missing artifact.
patterns.codeBlock:
# Force update snapshots and releases:
mvn clean install -U

# Check dependency tree:
mvn dependency:tree

patterns.rootCause: The Maven plugin `{pluginId}` was not found in any configured repository.
patterns.prescription: Verify the plugin groupId, artifactId, and version in pom.xml. Ensure the Maven Central repository is configured and accessible.
patterns.codeBlock:
<plugin>
    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
    <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>3.11.0</version>
</plugin>

patterns.rootCause: Maven compilation failed at {file}:{line}:{column} - {message}
patterns.prescription: Fix the compilation error reported at the specified location. Check the error message for details on what went wrong.

patterns.rootCause: Gradle could not resolve the dependency `{dependency}`.
patterns.prescription: Verify the dependency coordinates in build.gradle, ensure the correct repositories are configured (mavenCentral, google), and check network connectivity.

patterns.rootCause: The Gradle task `{taskName}` was not found in {project}.
patterns.prescription: Run `./gradlew tasks` to list all available tasks. Check for typos in the task name or ensure the required plugin is applied.
patterns.codeBlock:
# List all available tasks:
./gradlew tasks --all

# Run the correct task:
./gradlew build

patterns.rootCause: Spring could not find a bean of type `{beanType}` in the application context.
patterns.prescription: Ensure the class is annotated with @Component, @Service, @Repository, or @Configuration. Verify component scanning includes the package. Check that the bean is defined in a configuration class if manual registration is needed.

patterns.match: The dependencies of some of the beans in the application context form a cycle[:\s]*(.*)
patterns.rootCause: Spring detected a circular dependency between beans: {cycle}
patterns.prescription: Break the circular dependency by using @Lazy on one of the injected dependencies, refactoring to use setter injection, or extracting common logic into a separate bean.

patterns.match: No qualifying bean of type '(\S+)' available: expected (?:at least|single matching) (\d+|bean) but found (\d+|none)
patterns.rootCause: Spring autowiring failed for type `{beanType}`: expected {expected} but found {found}.
patterns.prescription: If multiple beans exist, use @Qualifier to specify which one. If no beans exist, ensure the class is annotated and scanned. Use @Primary to designate a default bean.

patterns.match: (?:Ambiguous handler methods mapped|There is already '(\w+)' bean method.*mapped|No mapping for (GET|POST|PUT|DELETE) (\S+))
patterns.rootCause: Spring MVC request mapping conflict or missing mapping for the requested endpoint.
patterns.prescription: Ensure each endpoint has a unique combination of HTTP method and path. Check for duplicate @RequestMapping annotations and verify the controller is scanned.

patterns.match: Cannot determine embedded database driver class for database type (NONE)?
patterns.rootCause: Spring Boot cannot determine the embedded database type. No database driver or configuration was found.
patterns.prescription: Add a database driver dependency to your project and configure the datasource in application.properties/application.yml.
patterns.codeBlock:
# application.properties:
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/mydb
spring.datasource.username=root
spring.datasource.password=secret
spring.datasource.driver-class-name=com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver
